root_text.sn55.34:1.2: “Cattārimāni, bhikkhave, devānaṁ devapadāni avisuddhānaṁ sattānaṁ visuddhiyā apariyodātānaṁ sattānaṁ pariyodapanāya.
root_text.sn55.34:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.34:2.3: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.34:2.4: Idaṁ paṭhamaṁ devānaṁ devapadaṁ avisuddhānaṁ sattānaṁ visuddhiyā apariyodātānaṁ sattānaṁ pariyodapanāya.
root_text.sn55.34:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.34:4.2: Idaṁ catutthaṁ devānaṁ devapadaṁ avisuddhānaṁ sattānaṁ visuddhiyā apariyodātānaṁ sattānaṁ pariyodapanāya.
root_text.sn55.34:4.3: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, cattāri devānaṁ devapadāni avisuddhānaṁ sattānaṁ visuddhiyā apariyodātānaṁ sattānaṁ pariyodapanāyā”ti.

translation_text.sn55.34:1.2: “Mendicants, these four footprints of the gods are in order to purify unpurified beings and cleanse unclean beings.
translation_text.sn55.34:2.2: Firstly, a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.34:2.4: This is the first footprint of the gods in order to purify unpurified beings and cleanse unclean beings.
translation_text.sn55.34:3.1: Furthermore, a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the teaching …
translation_text.sn55.34:4.1: Furthermore, a noble disciple’s ethical conduct is loved by the noble ones, unbroken, impeccable, spotless, and unmarred, liberating, praised by sensible people, not mistaken, and leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.34:4.2: This is the fourth footprint of the gods in order to purify unpurified beings and cleanse unclean beings.
translation_text.sn55.34:4.3: These four footprints of the gods are in order to purify unpurified beings and cleanse unclean beings.”
